intro_templates: It's a dog! Dogs bark! Can you imitate the sound a dog makes?
Look, a dog! It has soft fur. What color is it?
A little dog! The dog likes to play. Is it big or small?
Hello, doggy! Let's learn about our furry friend! Can you point to its ears?
Wow, a dog! I like dogs. Do you like dogs? Let's see what it can do!

facts.text: Dogs say 'woof woof' or 'ruff ruff'!
Dogs have soft, furry coats that you can pet.
Dogs wag their tails when they are happy.
Dogs walk on four legs and have a tail.
A dog's nose is always wet and cool to touch.

questions.text: What sound does a dog make?
How many legs does a dog have?
What does a dog do with its tail when it's happy?
questions.hint: It moves it back and forth!

questions.text: What color is this dog?
Is a dog's nose wet or dry?

intro_templates: It's a dog! Dogs are great pets. Do you know that their sense of smell is extremely sensitive?
Look at this dog! Dogs have been best friends with humans for a very long time.
Here's a dog! Did you know dogs can understand our feelings? They're very smart friends.
Check out this dog! Dogs aren't just cute; they have amazing skills, like being super smellers!
Meet this dog! Dogs love to be part of a family. What do you think they like to do with us?

facts.text: A dog's sense of smell is super powerful - much better than a human's!
Dogs love to play fetch! They will chase a ball and bring it back to you.
Dogs pant with their tongues out to cool down when they are hot.
Puppies are baby dogs. They are very small and like to sleep a lot.
Dogs can understand some human words, especially their name and commands like 'sit'.

questions.text: Why is a dog's nose so good at smelling?
questions.hint: Think about how they find hidden treats!

questions.text: What game do many dogs love to play with a ball?
questions.hint: Throw it, and they bring it back!

questions.text: Why does a dog stick out its tongue and pant?
questions.hint: What do you do when you feel hot?

questions.text: What is a baby dog called?
Can a dog learn its name?
questions.hint: What happens when you call a dog's name?

intro_templates: It's a dog! Do you know that different breeds of dogs can perform different tasks?
Look at this dog! Dogs are not just good at sniffing out scents; they can also help us in many ways!
This is a dog. But long, long ago, its ancestors were wild. Let's explore how they became 'man's best friend'.
Here we have a dog. Each dog is unique, not just in looks, but even in its nose print! Let's discover more.
Consider this dog. It communicates a lot without barking, using its body. Can you read its signals?

facts.text: Some dogs help people who cannot see. They are called guide dogs.
Dogs communicate with their bodies. A wagging tail often means happy, but a tucked tail can mean scared.
Dogs are descended from wolves, but now they are our friends and live in our homes.
A dog's whiskers help it feel things in the dark, like a cat's whiskers do.
Just like people have fingerprints, every dog's nose has a unique pattern of bumps and lines!

questions.text: What are the names of the dogs that can help blind people?
questions.hint: Think about crossing the street safely.

questions.text: If a dog's tail is between its legs, how might it be feeling?
questions.hint: How do you act when you feel shy or afraid?

questions.text: What animal were dogs' great-great-great grandparents?
questions.hint: They howl at the moon and live in packs.

questions.text: Why are whiskers important for a dog?
questions.hint: Think about walking in a dark room.

questions.text: Is every dog's nose print the same, like our fingerprints?
questions.hint: Are your fingerprints the same as your friend's?

intro_templates: It's a cat! Cats say 'meow'! Can you meow like a cat?
Look, a cat! It has soft fur. What color is it?
A little cat! The cat likes to sleep. Is it big or small?
Hello, kitty! Let's see our furry friend! Can you point to its tail?
Wow, a cat! I like cats. Do you like cats? Let's see what it does!

facts.text: Cats say 'meow' and 'purr'.
Cats have soft, furry coats.
Cats walk on four small feet.
Cats have long whiskers on their face.
Cats can climb up trees very well.

questions.text: What sound does a cat make?
How many legs does a cat have?
What does a cat have on its face?
What color is this cat?
Can cats climb trees?

intro_templates: It's a cat! Cats are great pets. Do you know they can see well in the dark?
Look at this cat! Cats have been living with humans for thousands of years.
Here's a cat! Did you know cats use their whiskers to feel things around them?
Check out this cat! Cats aren't just cute; they are amazing jumpers and climbers!
Meet this cat! Cats like to be clean and groom themselves often. How do they do it?

facts.text: Cats can see very well in the dark, much better than people can.
A cat's whiskers help it feel things and know if it can fit through spaces.
Cats groom themselves by licking their fur with their rough tongues.
Kittens are baby cats. They are born with their eyes closed.
Cats can make more than 100 different sounds to communicate.

questions.text: Why can cats see well in the dark?
questions.hint: Think about their eyes in dim light!

questions.text: What do cats use their whiskers for?
How does a cat clean its fur?
What is a baby cat called?
Can cats make many different sounds?

intro_templates: It's a cat! Do you know that cats' ancestors lived in the wild and were great hunters?
Look at this cat! Cats have a special way of landing on their feet when they fall.
This is a cat. Its rough tongue has a special job when it grooms itself. Let's find out!
Here we have a cat. Cats can communicate with their tails and ears, not just their voices.
Consider this cat. Even though they are pets now, they still have some wild instincts.

facts.text: Cats have a special 'righting reflex' that helps them land on their feet when they fall.
A cat's rough tongue has tiny hooks that help it clean fur and get meat off bones.
Cats' ancestors were wild animals that lived in Africa and the Middle East.
When a cat's tail is puffed up, it is scared. When it is straight up, it is happy.
Just like snowflakes, every cat's nose has a unique pattern of bumps and ridges.
A cat's pupils change shape with light: thin slits in the day, big and round at night.
Cats walk quietly because they have thick pads on their feet.
A cat's whiskers can sense the environment, like a tiny ruler.
Cats love to eat fish and mice because these foods help their eyesight.
Cats rarely get hurt when falling from high places, thanks to their pads and balance.

questions.text: How do cats usually land when they fall from a height?
Why is a cat's tongue rough like sandpaper?
Where did the first pet cats come from long ago?
questions.hint: A hot, dry part of the world.

questions.text: If a cat's tail is puffed up like a brush, how does it feel?
Are any two cats' nose prints exactly the same?
Why do a cat's eyes look different in the day and at night?
questions.expected_answer: To adapt to changes in light
questions.hint: Think about bright and dark places.

questions.text: Why do cats walk so quietly?
questions.expected_answer: They have soft pads on their feet
questions.hint: Look at their soft paw pads.

questions.text: What is the function of a cat's whiskers?
questions.expected_answer: To sense direction and measure width

questions.text: What food do cats like best?
questions.hint: Think about food that's good for their eyes.

questions.text: Why don't cats get hurt easily when jumping from high places?
questions.expected_answer: Their pads absorb shock and their bodies keep balance
questions.hint: They adjust their posture to land safely.

intro_templates: It's a rabbit! Rabbits hop-hop-hop. Can you hop too?
Look, a bunny! It has very long ears. Can you see them?
A fluffy rabbit! Its tail looks like a cotton ball.
Hello, bunny! It likes to nibble veggies. What color is it?
Wow, a rabbit! Let's count its ears and legs together.

facts.text: Rabbits have extra-long ears that stand up tall.
A rabbit moves with quick hops using its strong back legs.
Rabbits have a tiny tail that looks like a cotton puff.
A rabbit's nose wiggles when it sniffs.
Rabbits love crunchy carrots and leafy greens.

questions.text: How does a rabbit move?
How many ears does a rabbit have?
What shape is a rabbit's tail?
What food do rabbits like to nibble?
What wiggles on a rabbit's face when it smells?

intro_templates: It's a rabbit! Rabbits dig burrows to feel safe.
Look at this bunny! Its long ears help it listen for danger.
Here's a rabbit! It has big back legs for fast hops.
Check out this rabbit! It chews plants all day to stay healthy.
Meet this rabbit! It can turn its ears to catch sounds.

facts.text: Rabbits have strong back legs that let them leap far.
A rabbit's long ears can turn like little antennas to hear better.
Rabbits chew plants because their teeth keep growing.
Many rabbits sleep in burrows underground called warrens.
Baby rabbits are born with closed eyes.

questions.text: Why do rabbits keep chewing plants?
Why do rabbits move their long ears?
Where do rabbits like to sleep to feel safe?
Which legs are stronger on a rabbit?

intro_templates: It's a rabbit! Its teeth never stop growing, so it must gnaw every day.
Look at this rabbit! Its big ears also help cool it down.
This is a rabbit. It thumps its back foot to warn friends.
Here we have a rabbit. Its eyes are on the sides to watch for danger.
Consider this rabbit. Its fur color helps it hide from predators.

facts.text: A rabbit's ears can release heat, working like natural fans.
Rabbits thump their back feet to signal 'danger' to other rabbits.
Side-placed eyes give rabbits a wide view to spot sneaky predators.
Many wild rabbits change fur shades to blend with seasons.
Rabbits are crepuscular, most active at dawn and dusk.
A rabbit's long ears can turn to collect sounds and detect danger.
Rabbits get water from eating lots of fresh grass and leaves.
One of a rabbit's favorite vegetables is carrot.
Rabbits have long, strong back legs and can run very fast.
Rabbits have a split upper lip (three-part mouth) that helps them nibble grass and veggies.

questions.text: How can rabbit ears help in hot weather?
What does a rabbit thump mean?
questions.hint: A loud foot drum to alert friends.

questions.text: Why are a rabbit's eyes on the sides?
How does fur color help a rabbit?
When are rabbits most active?
Why are a rabbit's ears so long?
questions.expected_answer: To hear better and help cool off

questions.text: Why do rabbits rarely drink water directly?
questions.expected_answer: Their food contains lots of water
questions.hint: They mostly eat fresh grass and veggies.

questions.text: What vegetable do rabbits like best?
Why can rabbits run so fast?
questions.expected_answer: Their back legs are long and strong

questions.text: What is the benefit of a rabbit's split upper lip?
questions.expected_answer: It helps them nibble grass and food
questions.hint: It moves a lot when eating.

intro_templates: It's an elephant! Look at its long trunk. Can you pretend to swing a trunk?
Wow, an elephant! It's huge. Is it bigger than a car?
Hello, elephant! Its ears are like big fans. Can you flap your arms like ears?
A giant elephant! It has tusks like curved teeth. Can you spot them?
An elephant friend! Its feet go stomp-stomp. Can you make a stomp sound?

facts.text: Elephants have a long trunk they use like a super arm.
An elephant is one of the biggest land animals.
Elephants have big ears shaped like giant leaves.
An elephant's feet make loud stomps when it walks.
Elephants like to spray water with their trunks.

questions.text: What is the elephant's long nose called?
Is an elephant big or small?
What shape are an elephant's ears like?
What sound do elephant feet make when walking?
What does an elephant do with water?

intro_templates: It's an elephant! Its trunk can pick up tiny things and big logs.
Look at this elephant! Its ears help it cool down.
Here's an elephant! It lives in families called herds.
Check out this elephant! It uses tusks to dig and move branches.
Meet this elephant! It can drink lots of water at once.

facts.text: An elephant's trunk has thousands of muscles, strong enough to lift logs.
Elephants flap big ears to cool their bodies like fans.
Elephants live in herds led often by an older female called a matriarch.
Tusks are long teeth; elephants use them to dig for water and bark.
Baby elephants are called calves and drink milk from their mothers.

questions.text: How do elephants cool their big bodies?
What are elephant tusks and what do they do?
questions.expected_answer: long teeth used to dig/move things

questions.text: How strong is an elephant's trunk?
questions.expected_answer: can lift heavy logs/pick tiny things

intro_templates: It's an elephant! It can feel sounds through the ground with its feet.
Look at this elephant! It remembers water holes for years.
This is an elephant. Its deep rumbles travel far, like secret calls.
Here we have an elephant. Its skin wrinkles help keep moisture.
Consider this elephant. It uses mud baths like sunscreen.

facts.text: Elephants can sense low sounds through their feet, almost like hearing with the ground.
Elephants remember safe paths and water spots for many years.
Their rumbles are so low humans might not hear, but other elephants do far away.
Wrinkled skin holds water and mud, helping elephants stay cool.
A mud bath works like sunscreen and bug spray for elephants.

questions.text: How can elephants feel sounds without using ears?
Why do elephants take mud baths?
What helps elephants remember where water is?
How far can elephant rumbles travel?
Why does wrinkled skin help elephants?

intro_templates: It's a lion! Lions roar loudly. Can you roar like a lion?
Look, a lion! It has a fluffy mane. Do you see the mane?
A big lion! Its tail ends with a cute tuft of hair.
Hello, lion! It walks with big paws. Can you stomp like it?
Wow, a lion! People call it the king of the jungle.

facts.text: Lions can roar so loud you can hear it far away.
Male lions have a mane, a big fluffy collar of hair.
A lion's tail ends with a furry tuft.
Lions walk on four strong legs with big paws.
Lion cubs are playful and chase each other's tails.

questions.text: What loud sound does a lion make?
What is the fluffy hair around a male lion's head called?
How many legs does a lion have?
What is at the end of a lion's tail?

intro_templates: It's a lion! Lions live in groups called prides.
Look at this lion! Its roar can warn others away.
Here's a lion! Lions rest many hours in the day.
Check out this lion! Lionesses hunt together.
Meet this lion! Its mane can make it look bigger.

facts.text: Lions live in social groups called prides.
A lion's roar can travel several kilometers.
Lionesses often hunt together to catch food.
Lions rest up to 20 hours a day to save energy.
A mane can protect a male lion's neck during fights.

questions.text: What is a lion family group called?
How far can a lion's roar be heard?
Who usually leads the lion group in hunting? Male lion or female lion?
Why do lions sleep so much?
What does a mane help protect?

intro_templates: It's a lion! Its mane can show age and strength.
Look at this lion! Prides share food with cubs first.
This is a lion. Its roar is like a long-distance message.
Here we have a lion. Its tail tuft can help signal moves.
Consider this lion. Lions work together like a team.

facts.text: Darker, fuller manes can signal a stronger or older male lion.
In a pride, cubs often get to eat first to grow strong.
A lion's roar helps tell other lions: 'This is our space.'
Lions flick tails and body-posture to signal when to move or chase.
Team hunting lets lions bring down larger prey than one lion could alone.

questions.text: What can a dark mane tell other lions?
Who eats first in many prides?Adult lion or lion cub?
What message does a lion roar send?
How do lions signal each other during a hunt?
Why do lions hunt together?

intro_templates: It's a tiger! Look at its black stripes. Can you count some?
Wow, a tiger! It is big and orange. What color are the stripes?
Hello, tiger! It moves quietly. Can you tiptoe like it?
A strong tiger! It has a long tail. Can you spot it?
A tiger friend! Tigers growl. Can you make a low growl?

facts.text: Tigers have bold black stripes on orange fur with a white belly.
Each tiger has a unique stripe pattern like a fingerprint.
Tigers walk on four strong legs and have big paws.
Tigers can growl and roar with deep voices.
Tiger cubs play and practice pouncing.

questions.text: What pattern is on a tiger's fur?
How many legs does a tiger have?
What sound can a tiger make?
What color is a tiger's belly?

intro_templates: It's a tiger! Tigers are powerful solo hunters.
Look at this tiger! Its stripes help it hide in tall grass.
Here's a tiger! It loves to swim more than most big cats.
Check out this tiger! It has strong jaws for biting.
Meet this tiger! It marks territory with scent scratches.

facts.text: Tigers are solitary hunters that usually hunt alone.
Stripes help tigers blend with shadows in grass and forests.
Tigers are strong swimmers and enjoy water.
A tiger's jaws and teeth are built to bite through thick hides.
Tiger cubs are born blind and depend on their mother.

questions.text: Do tigers usually hunt alone or in groups?
How do stripes help a tiger?
Can tigers swim?
What part makes a tiger's bite powerful?
How are tiger cubs when they are born?

intro_templates: It's a tiger! Its stripe pattern is unique like a barcode.
Look at this tiger! It uses scent and scratches to say 'this is my place'.
This is a tiger. Its deep chuff sound is a friendly greeting.
Here we have a tiger. Its whiskers help judge spaces in the dark.
Consider this tiger. It swims to cool off and sneak on prey.

facts.text: No two tigers have the same stripe pattern, even on their skin.
Tigers use scent marks and tree scratches to map their territory.
Long whiskers help tigers sense space, handy in the dark.
Swimming lets tigers cool down and get closer to prey quietly.

questions.text: Are tiger stripes the same on every tiger?
How does a tiger say 'this is my area'?
Why are whiskers useful to a tiger at night?
Why might a tiger swim during a hunt?

visual_features.size: very tall (tallest animal in the world)

intro_templates: It's a giraffe! Look at its long neck!
Wow, a giraffe! It is so tall. Can you see its spots?
A tall giraffe! It has a long neck and long legs.
Hello, giraffe! It has a cute tail with hair on the end.
Look up high! A giraffe eats leaves from trees.

facts.text: Giraffes are the tallest animals in the world.
Giraffes have long necks and long legs.
Giraffes have brown spots on their bodies.
Giraffes eat leaves from tall trees.
Giraffes walk with a slow, swaying gait.

questions.text: What is special about a giraffe's neck?
How many legs does a giraffe have?
What color are giraffe's spots?
questions.hint: They are darker than their body!

questions.text: What do giraffes eat?
Are giraffes tall or short?

intro_templates: It's a giraffe! Do you know why it has such a long neck?
Look at this giraffe! Its tongue is very special. It's long and dark!
Here's a giraffe! Did you know giraffes only need to sleep a little bit?
Check out this giraffe! Even baby giraffes are very tall when they are born!
Meet this giraffe! It uses its long neck to look out for danger.

facts.text: A giraffe's tongue is very long and dark purple, perfect for grabbing leaves.
Giraffes only need to sleep for about 30 minutes a day, often standing up.
A baby giraffe is called a calf, and it can stand up just one hour after birth.
Giraffes have big hearts to pump blood all the way up their long necks.
Each giraffe has a unique pattern of spots, like our fingerprints.

questions.text: Why is a giraffe's tongue long and dark?
questions.hint: Think about reaching leaves in the sun!

questions.text: How long do giraffes sleep each day?
What is a baby giraffe called?
Why do giraffes need such big hearts?
Are all giraffe spot patterns the same?

intro_templates: It's a giraffe! Did you know that despite its long neck, it has the same number of neck bones as we do?
Look at this giraffe! Their long necks are not just for eating; they also help them spot predators from far away.
This is a giraffe. They have a special way of drinking water that requires them to spread their legs wide.
Here we have a giraffe. Even though they are so tall, they can run very fast when they need to.
Consider this giraffe. Their spots are not just for show; they help them hide in the sunlight and shadows of trees.

facts.text: Giraffes have only seven bones in their neck, the same number as humans, but each bone is much longer.
When a giraffe drinks water, it must spread its front legs wide apart to reach the ground.
Giraffes can run up to 35 miles per hour for short distances, which is as fast as a car in the city.
The spots on a giraffe's skin help to camouflage them in the patterns of sunlight and shadows in the trees.
Giraffes use their long necks to engage in 'necking' fights, where they swing their necks to hit each other.

questions.text: How many neck bones do giraffes have compared to humans?
What does a giraffe do with its legs when it drinks water?
questions.hint: It has to bend down low!

questions.text: How fast can a giraffe run when it is scared?
questions.hint: As fast as a slow car!

questions.text: Why do giraffes have spot patterns on their skin?
questions.hint: Think about hiding in the trees!

questions.text: What might two male giraffes do with their long necks when they are competing?
questions.hint: They might fight by swinging them!

intro_templates: It's a monkey! Monkeys like to climb trees!
Look, a monkey! It has a long tail. Can you see its face?
A funny monkey! It swings from branch to branch.
Hello, monkey! Let's watch our playful friend!
Wow, a monkey! I like monkeys. Do you like monkeys?

facts.text: Monkeys have long tails that help them balance.
Monkeys use their hands and feet to climb trees.
Monkeys eat fruits like bananas and apples.
Monkeys live in trees in warm forests.
Monkeys make sounds like 'ooh ooh ah ah'.

questions.text: What does a monkey have that is long?
How many legs does a monkey have?
questions.hint: They walk and climb with them!

questions.text: What do monkeys like to eat?
Where do monkeys live?
Can monkeys climb trees?
questions.hint: They are very good at it!

intro_templates: It's a monkey! Monkeys are very smart and can use tools.
Look at this monkey! Did you know monkeys have thumbs like us?
Here's a monkey! Monkeys live in groups called troops.
Check out this monkey! Monkeys groom each other to be friends.
Meet this monkey! Some monkeys have cheek pouches to store food.

facts.text: Monkeys have thumbs that can grip things, just like human hands.
Monkeys live in big groups called troops, with many friends and family.
Some monkeys have special cheek pouches to store food for later.
Monkeys use grooming to clean each other and show friendship.
Monkey babies, called infants, cling to their mothers' backs for safety.

questions.text: What special part do monkeys have on their hands?
What is a group of monkeys called?
Why do some monkeys have cheek pouches?
How do monkeys show they are friends?
Where do baby monkeys ride?

intro_templates: It's a monkey! Did you know monkeys are our closest animal relatives?
Look at this monkey! Their intelligence helps them solve problems in the wild.
This is a monkey. Different monkey species have adapted to eat different foods.
Here we have a monkey. They communicate with sounds, faces, and body language.
Consider this monkey. Their prehensile tail acts like a fifth hand for grasping.

facts.text: Monkeys are primates, which means they are close relatives to humans.
Some monkeys have prehensile tails that can grab and hold objects like a hand.
Monkeys communicate with a variety of vocalizations, facial expressions, and gestures.
Different species of monkeys have different diets, including fruits, leaves, insects, and even small animals.
Monkeys use their intelligence to remember where food is and how to use simple tools.

questions.text: What animal group are monkeys part of, which is close to humans?
questions.hint: We are in the same group!

questions.text: What can some monkeys do with their tails?
How do monkeys talk to each other?
Why might different monkeys eat different things?
How do monkeys show they are smart?

visual_features.size: big and round (like a big teddy bear)

intro_templates: It's a panda! Look at its black and white fur!
Wow, a panda! It is so cute. Can you see its round body?
A panda! It eats bamboo all day.
Hello, panda! It has black patches around its eyes.
Look, a panda! It looks like a big teddy bear.

facts.text: Pandas have black and white fur.
Pandas eat bamboo most of the time.
Pandas have big, round bodies and round heads.
Pandas walk on four legs.
Pandas live in bamboo forests.

questions.text: What colors are a panda's fur?
questions.hint: Two colors, like a zebra but different pattern!

questions.text: How many legs does a panda have?
What do pandas eat?
questions.hint: It's a green plant that grows in forests!

questions.text: Where do pandas live?
questions.hint: In forests full of their favorite food!

questions.text: Are pandas big or small?
questions.hint: They look like big teddy bears!

intro_templates: It's a panda! Do you know that pandas spend most of their day eating?
Look at this panda! Even though they are big, they are very good climbers.
Here's a panda! Did you know baby pandas are born very small and pink?
Check out this panda! They have a special thumb-like bone to hold bamboo.
Meet this panda! Pandas are from China and are a special symbol there.

facts.text: Pandas spend about 12 hours a day eating bamboo to get enough energy.
Baby pandas are born very small, pink, and blind, and they weigh about as much as an apple.
Pandas have a special wrist bone that works like a thumb to help them hold bamboo.
Pandas are very good climbers and can climb trees even when they are adults.
Pandas are from China and are considered a national treasure there.

questions.text: How long do pandas eat each day?
What do baby pandas look like when they are born?
questions.hint: They are not black and white at first!

questions.text: What special bone helps pandas hold bamboo?
questions.expected_answer: a wrist bone like a thumb
questions.hint: It's not a real thumb, but it works like one!

questions.text: Can pandas climb trees?
What country are pandas from?
questions.hint: It's a big country in Asia!

intro_templates: It's a panda! Did you know that pandas are actually bears, but they don't hibernate like other bears?
Look at this panda! Their black and white coloring might help them hide in snowy and rocky places.
This is a panda. Even though they eat mostly plants, their digestive system is like that of a meat-eater.
Here we have a panda. They are very rare and people work hard to protect them.
Consider this panda. They communicate with each other using sounds and scent marks.

facts.text: Pandas are bears, but unlike most bears, they do not hibernate during the winter.
The black and white colors of a panda might help it camouflage in snowy mountains and dark forests.
A panda's digestive system is designed for meat, but they eat mostly bamboo, so they must eat a lot to get energy.
Pandas are an endangered species, which means there are not many left in the wild and they need protection.
Pandas use different sounds like bleats, honks, and growls to communicate with each other.

questions.text: Do pandas sleep all winter like some other bears?
questions.hint: They stay awake and keep eating!

questions.text: Why might pandas have black and white fur?
Why do pandas need to eat so much bamboo?
questions.expected_answer: their digestion is not efficient for plants
questions.hint: Their stomachs are meant for meat!

questions.text: What does 'endangered' mean for pandas?
questions.hint: They need our help to survive!

questions.text: How do pandas talk to each other?
questions.expected_answer: with sounds like bleats and honks

intro_templates: It's a cow! Cows say 'moo'! Can you moo like a cow?
Look, a big cow! It has spots on its body. What color is it?
A farm cow! Cows eat grass all day long.
Hello, cow! See its long tail that swishes flies away.
Wow, a cow! It is big and strong. Do you see its horns?

facts.text: Cows say 'moo' with a loud, deep voice.
Cows have four legs with hard feet called hooves.
Many cows are black and white with spots or patches.
Cows eat grass in the field all day long.
Cows have big, wet noses that feel soft and leathery.

questions.text: What sound does a cow make?
How many legs does a cow have?
What do cows like to eat?
questions.hint: They eat green plants in the field!

questions.text: What are a cow's feet called?
questions.hint: They are hard, not soft like paws!

questions.text: Is a cow's nose wet or dry?

intro_templates: It's a cow! Cows give us milk. Do you know where milk comes from?
Look at this cow! Cows have a special stomach that lets them eat grass.
Here's a cow! Did you know a baby cow is called a calf?
Check out this cow! Cows chew their food in a special way called 'chewing cud'.
Meet this cow! They live in groups called herds and like to be with friends.

facts.text: Mother cows make milk for their babies, and we can drink that milk too.
Cows have one big stomach with four parts to help digest tough grass.
A baby cow is called a calf. It can stand and walk soon after it is born.
Cows spend about 8 hours every day chewing their food, which is called chewing cud.
Cows are very social animals and feel happiest when they are with other cows.

questions.text: What important food do we get from cows?
questions.hint: We drink it white and cold!

questions.text: How many parts does a cow's stomach have?
questions.hint: It's one stomach with four rooms!

questions.text: What is a baby cow called?
What is it called when a cow chews its food again?
questions.hint: It brings food back to its mouth!

questions.text: Do cows like to live alone or with other cows?

intro_templates: It's a cow! Did you know cows help turn grass, which we can't eat, into food we can, like milk?
Look at this cow! Their special digestion is why they can live on a diet of just grass.
This is a cow. Different breeds of cows can look very different and are good for different things.
Here we have a cow. They have almost 360-degree vision to watch for danger while they eat.
Consider this cow. They remember faces and can form strong friendships with other cows.

facts.text: Cows are ruminants, which means they ferment grass in their stomach first before digesting it fully.
A cow's eyes are on the sides of its head, giving it very wide vision to spot predators.
There are over 800 different breeds of cows, like Holsteins for milk and Angus for beef.
Cows have an excellent sense of smell and can detect odors from several miles away.
Cows communicate with each other using different moos, and they even have moos for their own calves.

questions.text: Why can cows eat grass, but people cannot?
questions.hint: They have a special stomach system!

questions.text: How does having eyes on the side of its head help a cow?
questions.hint: It can see without turning its head much!

questions.text: What is one breed of cow that gives a lot of milk?
questions.hint: They are often black and white!

questions.text: If a cow smells something far away, what might it be trying to do?
questions.hint: Find food, friends, or sense danger!

questions.text: How might a mother cow call her own baby calf?
questions.hint: She uses her voice in a unique way!

intro_templates: It's a horse! Horses say 'neigh'! Can you make that sound?
Look, a horse! It has a long tail and mane. What color is it?
A big horse! Horses like to run fast. Is it big or small?
Hello, horse! Let's see our strong friend. Can you point to its hooves?
Wow, a horse! I like horses. Do you like horses? Let's see what it can do!

facts.text: Horses say 'neigh' and 'whinny'.
Horses have four strong legs with hard hooves.
Horses have a long tail and a mane on their neck.
Horses can run very fast on their legs.
Horses eat grass, hay, and sometimes carrots.

questions.text: What sound does a horse make?
How many legs does a horse have?
What does a horse have on its neck?
What color is this horse?
Can horses run fast?

intro_templates: It's a horse! Horses are strong animals. Did you know they can sleep standing up?
Look at this horse! Horses have been friends with humans for thousands of years.
Here's a horse! Did you know baby horses can walk soon after they are born?
Check out this horse! Horses aren't just fast runners; they are also very smart!
Meet this horse! Horses help people in many ways, like on farms and in sports.

facts.text: Horses can sleep both lying down and standing up, using a special leg lock.
A baby horse is called a foal, and it can stand and walk just a few hours after birth.
Horses have very large eyes that let them see almost all around their body.
Horses communicate with sounds like neighs and whinnies, and also with their ears and tail.
People ride horses for fun, for sport, and to help with work on farms.

questions.text: How can horses sleep standing up?
questions.hint: They have a special leg lock!

questions.text: What is a baby horse called?
Why do horses have such large eyes?
questions.hint: They need to watch for danger!

questions.text: How do horses communicate with each other?
questions.hint: They use sounds and body language!

questions.text: Can people ride horses?

intro_templates: It's a horse! Did you know horses were one of the first animals to be tamed by humans?
Look at this horse! Their hooves are actually like one big toenail that keeps growing.
This is a horse. They have a special way of showing feelings with their ears and face.
Here we have a horse. Even though they are big, they can be very gentle and friendly.
Consider this horse. Their long legs and strong heart make them perfect for running fast.

facts.text: Horses were domesticated by humans over 5,000 years ago and changed how people traveled and farmed.
A horse's hooves are made of a hard material like our fingernails, and they need to be trimmed regularly.
Horses show their mood with their ears: forward means curious, pinned back means upset or angry.
Horses have a very strong sense of hearing and can move their ears to pinpoint sounds.
Just like people are right or left-handed, horses can be right or left-hoofed, preferring one side for leading.

questions.text: How long ago did humans first tame horses?
What are horse hooves made of, and why do they need trimming?
What does it mean when a horse's ears are pinned back?
Why can horses hear so well?
questions.hint: They can move their ears to catch sounds!

questions.text: Can horses have a preferred side, like people being right or left-handed?
questions.hint: They might lead with one hoof!

visual_features.size: medium (about as big as a large dog)

intro_templates: It's a sheep! Sheep say 'baa'! Can you make that sound?
Look, a sheep! It has thick, woolly fur. It looks so soft!
A fluffy sheep! The sheep eats grass all day. Can you see its wool?
Hello, sheep! It has four legs and a small tail. What color is it?
Wow, a sheep! Sheep live on farms. They give us wool for warm clothes.

facts.text: Sheep say 'baa baa' when they talk.
Sheep have thick, woolly coats that keep them warm.
Sheep walk on four legs and have hooves.
Sheep eat grass and plants in fields.
Sheep live in groups called flocks.

questions.text: What sound does a sheep make?
How many legs does a sheep have?
What do sheep eat?
questions.hint: They eat green plants in fields!

questions.text: What covers a sheep's body to keep it warm?
Do sheep live alone or in groups?

intro_templates: It's a sheep! Sheep give us wool that is made into warm sweaters and blankets.
Look at this sheep! Did you know sheep have excellent memory and can recognize faces?
Here's a sheep! Sheep are very social animals and like to stay close to their friends.
Check out this sheep! Farmers shear their wool once a year, and then it grows back.
Meet this sheep! Baby sheep are called lambs, and they can walk soon after they are born.

facts.text: Sheep's wool is sheared (cut off) once a year to make yarn for clothes.
Sheep have rectangular pupils that give them wide-angle vision to spot danger.
A baby sheep is called a lamb, and it can stand and walk within minutes of birth.
Sheep are herd animals and feel scared and nervous when they are alone.
Sheep have no top front teeth, just a hard pad to help them bite grass.

questions.text: What do people make from sheep's wool?
Why do sheep have rectangular pupils in their eyes?
questions.hint: It helps them see predators coming!

questions.text: What is a baby sheep called?
How do sheep feel when they are alone?
questions.hint: They prefer to be in groups!

questions.text: Do sheep have top front teeth to bite grass?
questions.hint: They have a hard gum pad instead!

intro_templates: It's a sheep! Did you know sheep were among the first animals to be domesticated by humans?
Look at this sheep! Their wool grows continuously, just like our hair and nails do.
This is a sheep. They have a special four-chambered stomach to digest tough grass.
Here we have a sheep. Their thick wool not only keeps them warm but also helps them float in water!
Consider this sheep. Farmers use sheep to help manage fields because they eat weeds and fertilize the soil.

facts.text: Sheep were first domesticated by humans over 10,000 years ago for wool, meat, and milk.
Sheep have a four-chambered stomach that helps them digest tough grasses and plants.
Sheep's wool contains lanolin, a natural oil that makes it water-resistant and helps sheep stay dry.
Sheep are used in some places to graze and naturally control weeds without using chemicals.

questions.text: Why did humans first keep sheep long ago?
How many chambers are in a sheep's stomach to help digest grass?
What natural oil in sheep's wool helps keep them dry in rain?
How can sheep help farmers take care of fields without using weed killers?

intro_templates: It's a pig! Pigs say 'oink oink'! Can you oink like a pig?
Look, a pig! It has a round body. What color is it?
A cute piggy! It has a curly tail. See the tail?
Hello, pig! Let's look at the pig. Can you see its snout?
Wow, a pig! Pigs like to roll in mud. Do you know why?

facts.text: Pigs say 'oink oink' when they talk.
Pigs have a round body and short legs.
Pigs have a flat nose called a snout.
Pigs have a curly tail that wiggles.
Pigs like to roll around in the mud.

questions.text: What sound does a pig make?
How many legs does a pig have?
What shape is a pig's tail?
questions.hint: It looks like a little curl!

questions.text: What color is this pig?
Do pigs like mud?

intro_templates: It's a pig! Pigs are very smart animals, just like dogs!
Look at this pig! Its snout is not just cute; it's a super tool for finding food!
Here's a pig! Did you know pigs can't sweat like we do? That's why they like mud.
Check out this pig! Pigs are farm animals and give us bacon and ham.
Meet this pig! Baby pigs are called piglets and they are born in a litter.

facts.text: Pigs are very intelligent animals and can learn tricks, just like dogs.
A pig's snout is strong and flexible, perfect for digging in the ground to find food.
Pigs roll in mud to cool down because they do not have sweat glands like humans.
A baby pig is called a piglet, and they often have many brothers and sisters at once.
Pigs are omnivores, which means they eat both plants and small animals.

questions.text: Are pigs smart animals?
Why do pigs like to roll in mud?
What is a baby pig called?
What does a pig use its snout for?
Do pigs eat only plants?
questions.hint: They eat plants and sometimes small animals.

intro_templates: It's a pig! Despite their reputation, pigs are actually very clean animals when they can be.
Look at this pig! They have an excellent sense of smell, even better than some dogs.
This is a pig. In the wild, pigs live in groups called sounders, led by a female.
Here we have a pig. Their curly tails might look cute, but they serve a purpose too.
Consider this pig. They are important farm animals that provide us with many products.

facts.text: Pigs are naturally clean animals and will choose a specific spot far from their sleeping area to go to the bathroom.
A pig's sense of smell is so strong that it can find truffles, a special mushroom, deep underground.
Wild pigs live in groups called sounders, which are usually made up of female pigs and their young.
A pig's curly tail helps keep flies and other insects away from its body.
Pigs provide us with many products like pork, bacon, ham, and even some medicines.

questions.text: Are pigs clean or dirty animals by nature?
questions.hint: They keep their living area tidy.

questions.text: Why are pigs sometimes used to find truffles?
questions.expected_answer: they have a strong sense of smell

questions.text: What is a group of wild pigs called?
How does a pig's curly tail help it?
questions.hint: It swishes like a fly swatter.

questions.text: What are some products we get from pigs?

visual_features.size: medium (about the size of a big book)

intro_templates: It's a duck! Ducks say 'quack quack'! Can you quack like a duck?
Look, a duck! It has soft feathers. What color is it?
A little duck! The duck likes to swim. Is it in the water?
Hello, duck! Let's see our feathered friend! Can you point to its beak?
Wow, a duck! I like ducks. Do you like ducks? Let's see what it can do!

facts.text: Ducks say 'quack quack' when they talk.
Ducks have soft feathers and a flat beak.
Ducks have webbed feet to help them swim.
Ducks like to swim in water.
A baby duck is called a duckling.

questions.text: What sound does a duck make?
How many legs does a duck have?
What kind of feet does a duck have?
Where do ducks like to swim?
What is a baby duck called?

intro_templates: It's a duck! Ducks are great swimmers. Do you know why their feet are webbed?
Look at this duck! Ducks have special feathers that keep them dry in water.
Here's a duck! Did you know ducks can sleep with one eye open?
Check out this duck! Ducks eat plants and small insects from the water.
Meet this duck! Ducklings follow their mother in a line called a 'parade'.

facts.text: Ducks have special oil on their feathers that makes them waterproof.
A duck's webbed feet work like paddles to push water when they swim.
Ducks can sleep with one eye open to watch for danger.
Ducks use their flat beaks to scoop up plants and bugs from the water.
Ducklings can swim and find food just a day after they hatch.

questions.text: Why don't ducks get wet when they swim?
How do webbed feet help a duck swim?
How can a duck sleep and stay safe at the same time?
What does a duck eat with its flat beak?
Can ducklings swim soon after they hatch?

intro_templates: It's a duck! Did you know that ducks have three eyelids to protect their eyes underwater?
Look at this duck! Ducks can fly long distances when they migrate to warmer places.
This is a duck. Their quacks can echo, but did you know not all ducks quack the same?
Here we have a duck. They have a special gland near their tail that makes waterproof oil.
Consider this duck. Even though they seem calm, ducks have a complex social structure in their groups.

facts.text: Ducks have three eyelids: one for blinking, one for sleeping, and one clear eyelid for seeing underwater.
Some ducks fly thousands of miles to warm places in winter, which is called migration.
Only female ducks make the classic 'quack' sound; male ducks make softer, raspy sounds.
Ducks have a special gland near their tail that produces oil to waterproof their feathers.

questions.text: How many eyelids does a duck have, and why?
Why do some ducks fly very far in the fall?
Do all ducks quack the same way?
Where does the waterproof oil on a duck's feathers come from?

visual_features.size: small to medium (about knee-high to a child)

intro_templates: It's a chicken! Chickens say 'cluck cluck'! Can you make chicken sounds?
Look, a chicken! It has feathers. What color are its feathers?
A little chicken! It has two legs and a beak. Is it big or small?
Hello, chicken! Let's see our farm friend! Can you point to its beak?
Wow, a chicken! I like chickens. Do you like chickens? Let's see what it can do!

facts.text: Chickens say 'cluck cluck' and 'peep peep'.
Chickens have soft feathers all over their body.
Chickens walk on two legs and have two wings.
Chickens have a red comb on top of their head.
Chickens peck at the ground to find food.

questions.text: What sound does a chicken make?
How many legs does a chicken have?
What does a chicken have on its head?
What covers a chicken's body?
How does a chicken eat food from the ground?

intro_templates: It's a chicken! Chickens are farm birds. Do you know that they lay eggs we can eat?
Look at this chicken! Chickens have different names: a female is a hen, a male is a rooster.
Here's a chicken! Did you know chickens dust bathe to keep clean?
Check out this chicken! Chickens aren't just for eggs; they also eat bugs and worms!
Meet this chicken! Baby chickens are called chicks and they are very fluffy.

facts.text: Hens (female chickens) lay eggs that we can cook and eat for breakfast.
Roosters (male chickens) crow 'cock-a-doodle-doo' in the morning to wake everyone up.
Chickens take dust baths by rolling in dry dirt to clean their feathers and remove pests.
Chicks are baby chickens. They are born from eggs and are covered in soft down.
Chickens have excellent eyesight and can see colors, even ultraviolet light.

questions.text: What does a hen give us that we can eat?
questions.hint: They are oval and come from the hen!

questions.text: What sound does a rooster make in the morning?
Why do chickens roll in the dirt?
questions.expected_answer: to take a dust bath and get clean
questions.hint: It helps them stay clean and healthy!

questions.text: What is a baby chicken called?
Can chickens see well?

intro_templates: It's a chicken! Did you know that chickens are the closest living relatives to the mighty Tyrannosaurus Rex?
Look at this chicken! They have a special way of communicating with different clucks for different dangers.
This is a chicken. Their combs and wattles help them stay cool in hot weather.
Here we have a chicken. Even though they have wings, most chickens can only fly short distances.
Consider this chicken. They are social animals and have a 'pecking order' to organize their group.

facts.text: Chickens are the closest living relatives to the Tyrannosaurus Rex, a giant dinosaur.
Chickens have different alarm calls for different predators, like a hawk in the sky versus a fox on the ground.
The red comb and wattles on a chicken's head help to release extra body heat and keep them cool.
Chickens can only fly short distances, usually to reach a low perch or escape danger.

questions.text: What giant dinosaur are chickens closely related to?
questions.hint: A big, scary dinosaur with tiny arms!

questions.text: Why might a chicken make a different sound for a hawk than for a fox?
questions.expected_answer: to warn others about different dangers
questions.hint: Different predators come from different directions!

questions.text: How does a chicken's red comb help it on a hot day?
questions.expected_answer: It helps release heat and cool the chicken down.

questions.text: Can chickens fly long distances like many other birds?

intro_templates: It's a bird! Look, it has feathers!
Wow, a bird! It can fly in the sky.
A little bird! It has two wings and a beak.
Hello, birdie! Listen, it makes chirping sounds.
Look up! A bird sits on a tree branch.

facts.text: Birds have feathers all over their bodies.
Birds have two wings and can fly.
Birds make chirping or tweeting sounds.
Birds have a beak and two legs.
Birds lay eggs in nests.

questions.text: What covers a bird's body?
How many wings does a bird have?
What sound does a small bird make?
What does a bird use to eat?
Where do birds lay their eggs?

intro_templates: It's a bird! Do you know that not all birds can fly?
Look at this bird! Its beak shape tells us what it eats.
Here's a bird! Did you know birds build different kinds of nests?
Check out this bird! Some birds can mimic sounds they hear.
Meet this bird! Birds migrate long distances to find warm weather.

facts.text: Different birds have different shaped beaks for eating different foods.
Some birds, like penguins and ostriches, cannot fly but are still birds.
Birds build nests from twigs, grass, and mud to keep their eggs safe.
Baby birds hatch from eggs and are called chicks.
Many birds fly south for the winter to find warmer places, called migration.

questions.text: Why do birds have different shaped beaks?
questions.hint: Think about the food they eat!

questions.text: Can all birds fly high in the sky?
questions.hint: Some birds live on the ground!

questions.text: What materials do birds use to build nests?
What is a baby bird called?
Why do some birds fly to warmer places in winter?
questions.hint: It gets cold and food is scarce!

intro_templates: It's a bird! Did you know that feathers do more than just help them fly?
Look at this bird! Their hollow bones make them light enough to fly.
This is a bird. They have excellent eyesight to spot food from far away.
Here we have a bird. Some birds can use tools, like sticks, to get food.
Consider this bird. Their feathers are waterproof, which helps them stay dry in rain.

facts.text: Bird feathers are not just for flying; they also keep birds warm and help them stay dry.
Birds have hollow bones that make their bodies lighter for flying long distances.
Many birds have very good eyesight, which helps them spot small insects or seeds from high up.
Some smart birds, like crows, can use sticks as tools to get insects out of tree bark.
Birds' feathers have special oils that make them waterproof, so they don't get too wet in the rain.

questions.text: What are three things that feathers do for birds?
questions.expected_answer: help fly, keep warm, stay dry
questions.hint: Think about flying, weather, and water!

questions.text: Why are bird bones hollow?
How does good eyesight help a bird find food?
questions.hint: They can see tiny things from high up!

questions.text: What is an example of a bird using a tool?
How do birds stay dry when it rains?
